expectations.text: SPEC.md exists at the root of the output directory
no leftover scratch files (_spec.txt, _spec.html, _toc.md, _spec_part_*.md, _sections_index.md) in the output directory
the six top-level sections appear in this exact order: Overview, Lexical Elements (Tokens), Structure (Grammar), Semantics, Examples, Appendix
expectations.evidence: all six sections in correct order

expectations.text: TOML's `#` line comments are documented under Comments
all four string variants are documented: basic ("..."), multi-line basic ("""..."""), literal ('...'), and multi-line literal ('''...''')
integers are documented with decimal, hexadecimal (0x), octal (0o), binary (0b) prefixes and underscore separators
float literals include +/-inf and +/-nan as documented forms
all four datetime types are documented: Offset Date-Time, Local Date-Time, Local Date, Local Time
both standard tables ([table]) and inline tables ({key = value}) are documented as distinct grammar constructs
arrays of tables ([[name]]) are documented as a distinct grammar construct
dotted keys (a.b.c = value) are documented with their nesting / table-creation semantics
the Examples section contains at least three distinct TOML documents
the complex example exercises strings, integers, floats, booleans, datetimes, arrays, and tables
the spec produces zero `> **Ambiguity:**` callouts (TOML 1.0.0 is well-defined) OR each callout includes a clear resolution recommendation
